subject: Biology
title: Immunodeficiency: When Defenses Fail
description: Learn Immunodeficiency: When Defenses Fail in one minute.

hook.text: A child born without a working immune system must live in a sterile bubble—any germ could be fatal.

buildup.text: Primary immunodeficiencies are genetic. Secondary ones are acquired—like HIV destroying T cells.

discovery.text: SCID—severe combined immunodeficiency—leaves patients with no T or B cells at all.

twist.text: Gene therapy has cured some SCID patients by inserting a working copy of the defective gene.

climax.text: Bone marrow transplants can rebuild an immune system entirely by replacing defective stem cells.

punchline.text: No defense means any infection is a death sentence.

quiz.question: What is SCID?
options: A genetic disorder with no functional T or B cells
A type of allergy to all foods
